meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand
immediate.summary: The maximum value of the National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 contract was raised from $5.6 billion to $17 billion The post Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: Pentagon awards $7.1 million to expand U.S. production of cover glass for satellites
immediate.summary: Defense Production Act funding was awarded to Martin Materials Solutions, a small business that makes radiation-resistant cover glass used on spacecraft solar arrays and thermal-control systems The post Pentagon awards $7.1 million to expand U.S. production of cover glass for satellites appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.summary: BosonQ Psi Federal LLC (BQP) has secured SpaceWERX funding to advance the company’s quantum-assisted AI platform to improve space domain awareness (SDA). This is the company’s first federal contract. The [] The post BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: Two Americans Killed, One Missing, as Iran Attacks US Base in Jordan
immediate.summary: Two American service members were killed, and a third is missing after Iranian ballistic missiles and drones struck an air base in Jordan used by U.S. forces. The post Two Americans Killed, One Missing, as Iran Attacks US Base in Jordan appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Senior Space Force General: Iran Conflict Shows ‘Return to US Spectrum Dominance’
immediate.summary: From missile warning to satellite communications for drones, the Space Force has played a key, if often unseen, role in the U.S. conflict with Iran, the head of the service’s Combat Forces Command said July 17. And one area in particular has seen Guardians be especially active: the electromagnetic spectrum. The post Senior Space Force General: Iran Conflict Shows ‘Return to US Spectrum Dominance’ appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Space Force Offering $25K Bonus for 8-Year Enlistments, but Slots Are Limited
immediate.summary: Future Guardians willing to serve eight years for their first Active-Duty enlistment are now eligible for a $25,000 bonus—but the deal won’t last long, according to a recent Space Force announcement. The post Space Force Offering $25K Bonus for 8-Year Enlistments, but Slots Are Limited appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission

immediate.summary: As the U.S. war with Iran intensifies, the Pentagon is rushing more warplanes to the region, deploying Air Force F-16 Fighting Falcons and F-35 Lightning II fighters to the Middle East, people familiar with the matter told Air Space Forces Magazine. immediate.title: NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission
immediate.summary: NASA and Draper have terminated plans for a lunar lander mission after significant delays in development of the spacecraft. The post NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: Space Force Announces Enlisted Promotions as It Prepares to Switch to ‘Fully Qualified’ System
immediate.summary: The most recent Space Force enlisted promotion selection was the last to use the old board format for sergeants, as the service transitions to a “fully qualified” method of selecting its junior NCOs. The post Space Force Announces Enlisted Promotions as It Prepares to Switch to Fully Qualified System appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: New ‘Golden Defender’ missile monitor ship to be built at Hanwha Philly shipyard
immediate.summary: The ship, which will support the Golden Dome effort, will be based on the national Security Multi-Mission Vessels Hanwha Philly Shipyard builds for the US Maritime Administration.

immediate.title: 'The dawn of a new space era': Vikram-1, India's 1st private orbital rocket, aces debut launch
immediate.summary: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket aced its debut mission on Saturday morning (July 18), making India the third nation with a private orbital launch capability.

immediate.title: Space Force Chief Nominee: China Going ‘Breathtakingly Fast’ with Space Weapons
immediate.summary: China’s rapidly advancing capabilities in space are a major concern for the presumed next chief of U.S. Space Force, who will likely oversee rapid growth in manpower and equipment to counter the growing threat. The post Space Force Chief Nominee: China Going ‘Breathtakingly Fast’ with Space Weapons appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Space Development Agency Launches 21 Data Transport Satellites on SpaceX Rocket
immediate.summary: The Space Development Agency resumed its Tranche 1 launch effort July 16, sending 21 data transport satellites to continue building out a mesh network in low-Earth orbit. The post Space Development Agency Launches 21 Data Transport Satellites on SpaceX Rocket appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: SpaceX's Starship Flight 13 test launch aborts at last second (video)
immediate.summary: SpaceX aborted Flight 13 of its giant Starship rocket at the last second on Thursday (July 16). It's unclear when the company will try again.

same_day.summary: Using the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST), astronomers have gotten a glimpse at the mechanisms that supermassive black holes use to feed themselves.

same_day.summary: Japan's Hayabusa2 spacecraft returned spectacular images of a near-Earth asteroid following a super-close flyby on July 5, but heated debate was needed before teams signed off on the daring attempt.

same_day.summary: The island nation of Mauritius signed the Artemis Accords July 17, becoming the 70th nation to do so in less than six years. The post Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords appeared first on SpaceNews .

same_day.title: 2 US troops killed, 1 missing after Iranian missile, drone attack in Jordan
same_day.summary: Two U.S. service members were killed in action on Friday in an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack on U.S. and partner forces in Jordan.

same_day.summary: The U.S. Navy’s expensive effort to install hypersonic missiles on three Zumwalt-class destroyers is two years behind schedule.

same_day.title: Serbia signs the Artemis Accords
